turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having this persistent cough for two weeks now, it's really getting me down. It started out dry but now I'm producing yellowish-green sputum and it's just not going away.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The cough is really the worst part - it's like a constant ache in my chest, and I've been feeling pretty miserable overall. It's just so annoying and uncomfortable!

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about two weeks ago, I'd say around 10 days after I got back from spring break. At first, I thought it was just a cold or something, but then the cough just wouldn't go away...

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens all the time - every day, multiple times a day.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's an 8 out of 10 - I've been feeling pretty unwell lately and it's really affecting my daily life.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: I've noticed that when I cough, it can be a bit louder than usual and sounds different from normal. It's been happening every time I cough for the past week or so.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: It definitely gets worse when I exert myself, like when I'm teaching my students in the classroom or doing household chores.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: I've been having some night sweats lately - every single night for the past week, actually.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: I do have some chest pain when I cough or breathe deeply - it's sharp and on my right side. It's pretty uncomfortable!

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any swelling in my legs or feet.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've been taking some over-the-counter dextromethorphan for the cough, but it's only provided minimal relief.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any chronic medical conditions. I'm pretty healthy overall, just this persistent cough is really getting me down.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: No, I haven't had my cholesterol checked recently.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I've never smoked, but I do have a social glass of wine on weekends.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: I'd say my diet is pretty normal, lots of fruits and veggies, but I've been eating less since this cough started. As for exercise, I try to stay active by walking during lunch breaks, but it's been harder lately because I'm just not feeling up to it.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: No, my mom has asthma, that's about it.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My maternal grandmother passed away from lung cancer a few years ago. That's about the only other significant health issue I'm aware of in my family.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: It's been about a year since my last physical exam, everything checked out fine back then.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: I've been having some trouble sleeping lately and I'm feeling a bit hot at night.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: I've actually been feeling pretty fatigued lately, too. It's like I just don't have the energy to do even simple things, and it's making me really frustrated.
